rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluorocyclization of N-allylcarboxamides to 5-fluoromethyl-2-oxazolines mediated by anodically generated hypervalent aryl-iodine(III) species using Et3N·5HF as fluoride source
electrochemical_role: Anodic 2-electron oxidation of 4-methyliodobenzene (ArI) to a hypervalent ArI(III)/iodonium-type mediator that activates the alkene; HF provides fluoride and the cathode reduces protons

key_intermediates.name: anodically oxidized 4-methyliodobenzene (ArI cation/ArIF2-type hypervalent iodine(III) mediator)
alkene-coordinated iodonium (three-membered iodiranium) intermediate 4a from attack of allyl C=C on ArI(III)
5-(λ3-iodanyl)methyl oxazoline 4b formed by intramolecular ring-opening by the amide carbonyl oxygen
4-methyliodobenzene (ArI) released after SN2 displacement by fluoride

electron_flow: ArI is oxidized at the anode (2e-) to a hypervalent I(III) species that electrophilically activates the allyl alkene of the N-allylcarboxamide, forming an iodiranium; intramolecular attack by the amide carbonyl oxygen gives an iodanyl-methyl oxazoline, which undergoes SN2 displacement by fluoride (from Et3N·5HF) to release the fluoromethyl oxazoline and regenerate ArI.

probe_reagents.name: 4-tert-butyliodobenzene mediator
probe_reagents.observation: mediator recovered quantitatively after electrolysis, supporting catalytic ArI/ArI(III) cycle

probe_reagents.name: ex-cell electrolysis protocol
probe_reagents.observation: pre-generated ArI(III) reagent (ex-cell) converted 1h to 2h cleanly, avoiding side reactions seen in-cell — supports a discrete ArI(III) mediator pathway

notes: In-cell electrolysis of 2-phenyl substrate 1h gave intramolecular coupling side product; ex-cell (pre-generated ArI(III)) gave cleaner oxazoline 2h, indicating that direct anodic oxidation of substrate is a competing pathway suppressed when the hypervalent iodine mediator is used as a discrete reagent.
